summary.intent: Address nine unresolved Codex review findings on PR #727 (codex/issue-320-431-checkbox-adapters) covering adapter lint hardening, sketch validation, scaffold layout, E2E fixture CSS loading, Dioxus widget public assets, and reset defaults in widget demos.
summary.outcome: All nine Codex findings were addressed and committed as 'fix(workflow): address Codex adapter hardening findings'. All focused regression tests and lint gates passed before the commit.

repo: The Dioxus widget example public/ directories (widgets-dioxus, widgets-dioxus-css, widgets-dioxus-tailwind) contain symlinks pointing to the crate companion stylesheets (crates/ars-core/ars-base.css, crates/ars-interactions/ars-interactions.css) — writing to public/*.css files follows the symlink and damages the crate source.

Adapter parity lint was silently skipping components without usage docs instead of failing — the fix scopes delivery-hardening checks to workflow-enrolled components (those with usage docs or a single-component counterpart sketch).
Category lookup in the lint was hard-coded; it was replaced with manifest-derived data from spec/manifest.toml.
The helper/semantic-boundary regex was incorrectly matching public component functions (e.g., HiddenInput); it was narrowed to only match pub(...) scoped (private/internal) helpers.
Sketch validation was accepting header-only matrices as valid; the validator now rejects them.
Scaffold output was generating flat files; it now creates src/<category>/<component>/mod.rs (directory-backed module shape).
E2E fixtures for both Leptos and Dioxus were rendering the styled Checkbox without injecting its CSS, causing unstyled rendering in fixture tests.

The controlled required checkbox in all six widget examples (widgets-leptos, widgets-leptos-css, widgets-leptos-tailwind, widgets-dioxus, widgets-dioxus-css, widgets-dioxus-tailwind) was missing an explicit checked default matching the reset handler.

code.finding: Parity lint was filtering by docs presence before validating delivery — fixed to make missing docs a hard failure for workflow-scoped components
Presence checks were file-existence based; replaced with content-backed checks requiring actual test attributes
Helper detection regex was too broad, matching public component fns; narrowed to pub(...) visibility forms
Sketch validator accepted header-only matrices as valid; now rejects them
Scaffold was generating flat src files; changed to create src/<category>/<component>/mod.rs
New regression test added: dioxus_widget_public_assets_exist_for_inline_includes, resolving paths from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/.. to reach workspace root
Style injection for Checkbox CSS was missing from Leptos E2E fixture
Style injection for Checkbox CSS was missing from Dioxus E2E fixture

workflow: When git restore is sandboxed (metadata outside writable root), restoring files requires overwriting content via python3 or apply_patch rather than git commands.
Codex review threads are fetched via GitHub GraphQL API filtering on isResolved==false and author matching 'codex'.
The waiting-for-codex-review skill governs the loop: push fix → post '@codex review' comment → poll CI + Codex reaction/thread count.
Running cargo xfmt after each patch batch is necessary because rustfmt reformats surrounding code, invalidating the next patch's context lines.
Integration tests in xtask/tests/ run with xtask/ as their cwd assumption; paths to workspace-level files must use CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/..

friction: git restore was blocked by sandbox (git metadata lives outside the writable root), requiring multiple workarounds (apply_patch, python3 content overwrite) to restore accidentally modified crate CSS files.

apply_patch reported success creating Dioxus public/*.css files but they were silently not created — the writes went through symlinks back to crate source files, requiring filesystem investigation before the root cause was found.
Cargo xfmt reformatted surrounding code after each patch, causing subsequent patch context to not match — required re-reading sections and re-applying patches multiple times.

The adapter parity lint initially surfaced legacy pre-workflow adapters as failures after the stricter checks were added, requiring a scoping mechanism to limit new gates to workflow-enrolled components only.
The xtask integration test path resolution was wrong on first attempt (used xtask-relative paths instead of workspace-relative paths via CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/..).
open_items: Legacy adapters (those predating the workflow) are still counted for parity but not gated on the new delivery-hardening checks — they should eventually be enrolled in the workflow and have docs/tests added.

The public/ symlink strategy for Dioxus widget examples is implicit and fragile — the new regression test catches missing targets, but the symlink approach itself (vs. real files or a build step) may warrant documentation or reconsideration.
The existing third-party future-incompatibility clippy note was left unresolved (noted as pre-existing).
Codex review threads were not yet replied to or resolved in this transcript — the commit was staged but the reply/resolve loop was not shown as complete.
